contraindications.clinicalRationale: Combination therapy with ACEi, ARBs, or direct renin inhibitors was associated with higher rates of hyperkalemia and acute kidney injury compared to monotherapy, with no additional kidney or cardiovascular benefit demonstrated in long-term outcome trials
contraindications.riskDescription: Increased risk of hyperkalemia and acute kidney injury
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Use only one agent at a time to block the RAS - monotherapy with single RAS inhibitor
contraindications.sourceSnippet: combination therapy was associated with a higher rate of hyperkalemia and AKI, and thus only one agent at a time should be used to block the RAS
